company.description: Enkei is a Stockholm-based startup that transforms construction and ceramic demolition waste into ReCeramix™, a design-grade circular material that serves as an alternative to concrete, marble, and terrazzo for interior surfaces. Founded by Lovisa Sunnerholm and Miriam Bichsel, the company reduces cement use by up to 80% compared to conventional decorative concrete and is distributed through premium design venues including Nordiska Galleriet in Stockholm and The Oblist in Paris.
company.name: Enkei

company.description: Epoch's biorecycling platform unlocks circularity for the toughest-to-recycle materials. Our AI-designed enzymes enable infinite recycling for mixed plastics and textiles at low temperatures. We produce sustainable outputs for use in apparel, automotive, packaging and more.
company.name: Epoch Biodesign

company.description: Evotrex is a California-based company innovating outdoor travel with intelligent, power-generating RV trailers designed for adventure without limits, enabling off-grid living with advanced energy management and comfort.
company.name: Evotrex

company.description: Exergy3 is a University of Edinburgh spinout commercialising a modular high-temperature thermal-energy-storage (TES) technology — a "thermal battery" that stores heat at up to 1,200°C with significantly higher energy density than incumbent systems. The platform converts curtailed or surplus renewable electricity into clean industrial heat, replacing fossil-fuel boilers in sectors like distilling, food & beverage, chemicals, and manufacturing. Co-founded in May 2023 by CEO Dr Markus Rondé and CSO Dr Adam Robinson (technology inventor, School of Engineering), with a flagship first-of-a-kind deployment at the Annandale Distillery demonstrating real-world operation.
company.name: Exergy3

company.description: Exponent Energy develops a proprietary energy stack for 15-minute rapid EV charging. The stack comprises e^pack (battery pack), e^pump (charging station), and e^plug (connector) that together enable 0%-100% rapid charge in 15 minutes with a 3,000-cycle lifetime warranty using regular LFP cells.
company.name: Exponent Energy

company.description: Feldwerke is a Munich-based cleantech company developing and installing open-field agri-photovoltaic systems that pair single-axis solar trackers with active farming on the same parcels. Founded in 2023 by Nicolai Reiners, Nils Kuchenbuch and Marco Mielenz, the firm has installed more than 20 MW to date — including the largest agri-PV plant in southern Germany at Oberndorf am Lech — and is developing a pipeline of roughly 50 projects totaling over 400 MW with an ambition of 1 GW by 2030.
company.name: Feldwerke

company.description: Fermeate is a precision-fermentation company applying optogenetics to dynamically control microbial gene expression in industrial bioreactors, aiming to lower the cost of fermentation-derived ingredients without retrofitting existing tanks.
company.name: Fermeate

company.description: Foamlab is a Delft-based biomaterials startup developing high-performance, compostable foams made from bacterial nanocellulose as an alternative to fossil-based plastics like polystyrene. A 2024 spin-off from TU Delft's Biodesign Lab (Faculty of Industrial Design Engineering), the company uses microbial fermentation to grow foams whose mechanical, acoustic and aesthetic properties can be tuned at the nanoscale, targeting construction insulation, packaging, fashion and furniture.
company.name: Foamlab

company.description: Forest (operated by HumanForest) runs a shared electric bike service in London, which it has scaled into the world's largest shared e-bike market. Since launching in 2020, the company has grown to 1.5 million users completing roughly two million rides per month across 18 boroughs, and powers its fleet with zero-emissions energy while offering riders up to 30 free minutes of cycling daily.
company.name: Forest

company.description: A Solana-based Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network (DePIN) project building a decentralized energy grid where participants can produce, trade, and consume energy peer-to-peer.
company.name: Fuse Energy

company.description: GALVANY (GALVANY Energy GmbH) is a Berlin-based climate and energy-technology company, founded in 2022, that runs an end-to-end platform for residential heat pump adoption in Germany. It combines its own GALVANY Cube hardware (built with Panasonic in Pilsen), battery storage and the Fusion energy-management software with customer acquisition, subsidy handling, installation and ongoing operation, with a focus on older multi-family buildings. The company reached €20.1M revenue and positive EBIT in 2025 after installing more than 2,500 systems.
company.name: GALVANY

company.description: Powering a cleaner future | GPS Renewables is India's leading full-stack biofuels firm, offering technology & EPC to project development solutions for bioCNG, Ethanol & Green H2. GPSR is backed by marquee investors including the likes of SBI Capital (Neev), Hivos-Triodos and Caspian. GPSR has been a common factor behind many milestone biofuel projects, including the biogas projects that powered Indore & Panchgani to bag the cleanest city awards in their respective categories, or Reliance Bioenergy's milestone projects such as Barabanki.
company.name: GPSR Arya

company.description: GR3N is a Lugano-based Swiss cleantech company, founded in 2013, that develops chemical recycling technology for PET plastics and polyester fibres. Its patented MADE (Microwave Assisted DEpolymerisation) process can recycle 100% of PET waste — including textiles, films and coloured resins that conventional mechanical recycling cannot handle — into food-grade monomers that can be reused indefinitely. The company is industrialising the technology through MODUS, a first-of-a-kind 40,000-ton/year plant in Spain.
company.name: GR3N

company.description: Gel Coat Biomaterials is a University of Tokyo spinout commercializing Gel Coat™, a proprietary zwitterionic amoeba-hydrogel platform that encapsulates and stabilises enzymes, antibodies and nucleic acids without compromising activity. The technology lets fragile biomolecules survive heat, organic solvents and ambient storage, sharply lowering cost and complexity for biomanufacturers in food, chemicals and pharma. The company was founded by Professor Madoka Takai of the University of Tokyo Graduate School of Engineering and licenses Gel Coat™ to industrial partners.
company.name: Gel Coat Biomaterials

company.description: Gigaton (formerly Carbon Re) is a London-based AI company, spun out of UCL and the University of Cambridge, that builds self-learning control software for energy-intensive heavy industries such as cement, steel, glass and chemicals. Rather than sitting on top of legacy systems, its platform replaces the underlying control stack, autonomously adjusting parameters like fuel mix, kiln speed and oxygen to cut energy use, emissions and process instability. It is already deployed by major cement producers including Adani Cement, Heidelberg Materials and Holcim, delivering seven-figure annual savings per plant.
company.name: Gigaton

company.description: Goshe Energy Storage is a Boulder, Colorado developer, owner, and operator of utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS), focused on acquiring late-stage projects and managing them through construction and commercial operations. The company targets the ERCOT Texas market, having raised over $460 million in total capital to support its first wave of grid-scale BESS assets.
company.name: Goshe Energy Storage

company.description: Granarium Technologies is a Finnish deep-tech startup spun out of VTT Technical Research Centre that develops 100% renewable supercapacitors for fast-response energy storage. Its patented platform uses nanocellulose and biocarbon from waste wood and agricultural residues, cutting production capex by up to 80% versus conventional supercapacitors while avoiding finite, mined materials. The devices provide millisecond-scale grid balancing, frequency response and industrial power-quality support, complementing batteries for short-duration needs.
company.name: Granarium Technologies

company.description: Green Genius is a renewable energy company based in Lithuania, specializing in the development of large-scale solar power projects and battery energy storage systems.
company.name: Green Genius

company.description: Greenpixie is a UK-based energy SaaS startup that helps Fortune 1000 enterprises reduce the carbon, water, and cost impact of cloud and AI infrastructure. Its FinOps and GreenOps tooling integrates with major cloud providers to terminate zombie resources, optimise AI model usage, and route workloads to low-carbon regions. Customers include Mastercard.
company.name: Greenpixie

company.description: GridCARE develops physics-based AI software that helps utilities and AI data center operators identify latent capacity on existing power grids, compressing interconnection timelines from years to months. Founded at Stanford's Doerr School of Sustainability, its Energize platform models grid congestion, outages, weather and demand variability in real time to activate underutilized megawatts for AI factories.
company.name: GridCARE

company.description: Haga Bioscience (formerly HAGA.BIO) is a Stockholm-based spatial biology company founded in 2024 that specializes in spatial RNA biomarker discovery and clinical translation. Its in situ hybridization technology enables sensitive, specific detection of single nucleotide variants on RNA directly in tissue, aiming to bridge the gap between biomarker discovery and validation across large clinical cohorts. The company positions its platform as a cost-effective, scalable approach to overcoming sample-throughput and cost limitations in translational spatial transcriptomics.
company.name: Haga Bioscience

company.description: Halo Drive is a Glasgow-based startup building vehicle-aware AI for commercial fleets. Its technology pairs on-vehicle intelligence (Halo Core) with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) networking and a fleet platform, ViewPort, that connects vehicles to live energy, grid, weather, infrastructure and traffic data to coordinate movement, charging and deployment. The company targets fleet operators and OEMs with autonomy and energy-optimisation capabilities, including vehicle-to-grid use of idle assets.
company.name: Halo Drive

company.description: Helical Fusion is a private fusion energy developer based in Japan focused on commercializing fusion power using a stellarator design. The company aims to build the world's first commercially viable steady-state net power fusion plant by leveraging Japan's advanced manufacturing capabilities and decades of fusion research, particularly from the National Institute for Fusion Science and its Large Helical Device facility. Their Helix Program includes pilot plants Helix HARUKA and Helix KANATA, tar
company.name: Helical Fusion

company.description: Helion is an Everett, Washington-based fusion energy company developing systems to generate zero-carbon electricity from nuclear fusion. Led by CEO David Kirtley, it is building toward grid-scale commercial fusion and has signed power agreements with Microsoft and steelmaker Nucor. It is among the most heavily funded private fusion developers.
company.name: Helion

company.description: Helix Earth develops accelerated liquid–gas chemistry hardware derived from NASA aerospace research to improve commercial HVAC performance, humidity management, industrial filtration, and CO2 capture. The company’s retrofit systems enable rapid liquid–gas reactions to reduce energy use and lower the cost and footprint of capture equipment, targeting building owners, facility managers, and industrial operators. Headquartered in Houston, Helix Earth’s technology originated from Rice University research and is positioned for manufacturing scale-up and commercial deployment.
company.name: Helix Earth

company.description: Hexigone Inhibitors is a Welsh advanced-materials company that makes smart anti-corrosion additives for protective coatings used on metal assets in defence, marine and industrial settings. Its patented Intelli-ion technology releases inhibitor only when corrosion begins, working at low dosages to cut cost and carbon while reducing reliance on heavy metals and toxic chemistries. The company is spun out of the West Wales materials ecosystem and supported by the Welsh Government.
company.name: Hexigone

company.description: HIT Mag is a French deeptech startup based in Plouzané, Brittany, spun out of the Lab-STICC laboratory (UBO/CNRS) in Brest. It develops and manufactures high-performance permanent magnets free of rare earths, using a dual materials-and-process innovation aimed at securing a sovereign, eco-friendly European magnet supply chain at competitive cost. Its magnets target automotive and electric mobility, robotics, wind energy, telecommunications and space, with CNES as an early customer for space applications.
company.name: Hit Mag

company.description: Hygenco Green Energies is an Indian green hydrogen and green ammonia developer founded in 2020 and headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana. It builds and operates commercial-scale production assets on a Hydrogen-as-a-Service model, supplying zero-emission green molecules to hard-to-abate industrial customers; its Project Heartland in Hisar was among India's first 100% green hydrogen plants. The company is scaling a portfolio of green hydrogen and green ammonia projects, including a 1.1 million tonne-per-year green ammonia facility in Odisha.
company.name: Hygenco Green Energies

company.description: INXM is a Berlin-based enterprise AI company, founded in 2025, that builds an AI Process Execution Engine for industrial and business operations. Its "Compiled AI" approach uses large language models to generate deterministic, auditable workflow code that runs reliably across a company's existing ERP, MES and PLM systems rather than replacing them. The founding team comprises former chief digital officers of Isar Aerospace and Volocopter alongside veterans of n8n and Thoughtworks, targeting manufacturers and other regulated industries that need repeatable, compliant automation.
company.name: INXM

company.description: IREN (NASDAQ: IREN) is a vertically integrated AI Cloud provider operating large-scale data centers and GPU clusters for AI training and inference. Its platform is underpinned by an expansive portfolio of grid-connected land and renewable power across the U.S. and Canada, originally built up under the Iris Energy name before the February 2024 rebrand. The company designs and deploys direct-to-chip liquid cooling and serves AI-native and enterprise customers from bare metal through Kubernetes.
company.name: IREN

company.description: Iceotope is a UK-based provider of precision liquid cooling technology for AI infrastructure, high-performance computing and edge data center deployments. Its chassis-based system replaces traditional air cooling with liquid-based thermal management, enabling rack densities beyond the limits of air or direct-to-chip cooling while reducing energy and water use. Founded in 2005 in Sheffield, the company holds more than 200 granted and pending patents.
company.name: Iceotope

company.description: Immutrin is a biotechnology company developing next-generation antibody therapies designed to clear systemic amyloid deposits and reverse amyloidosis. Its lead asset targets ATTR cardiomyopathy, a severe and progressive form of amyloidosis, and represents a potential best-in-class treatment engineered to selectively and specifically remove established amyloid fibrils.
company.name: Immutrin

company.description: Imperagen is a Manchester-based techbio company that engineers enzymes using a closed-loop platform combining quantum physics simulations, problem-specific AI models, and highly automated wet-lab robotics. Spun out of the University of Manchester's Manchester Institute of Biotechnology in 2021, it serves pharmaceutical manufacturing, personal care, sustainable fine chemicals, and industrial biotech customers, with case work delivering 500-700x improvements in enzyme productivity.
company.name: Imperagen

company.description: Integra Robotics, operated by Social Hardware International, is a Bengaluru-based deeptech company building a modular embodied-AI platform for Industry 5.0 and defence. Its portfolio spans collaborative and industrial robotic arms, end-of-arm tooling, unmanned ground vehicles, and advanced vision systems for manufacturing, warehouse automation, security, defence, and subsea use. A shared communication, teleoperation, and autonomous-learning stack underpins a human-in-the-loop data flywheel that turns guided tasks into training data so workflows grow more autonomous over time.
company.name: Integra Robotics

company.description: Integrant is a Sydney-based Australian biotechnology company founded in 2015 that develops AI-enhanced regenerative medicine and orthopaedic surgical products for bone healing, fracture management, and cartilage regeneration. Its flagship intra-articular injection therapy targets meniscus cartilage repair as a non-invasive, disease-modifying treatment for osteoarthritis, and its proprietary machine-learning platforms are used to design biomaterials, tailor regenerative protocols to individual patients, and predict clinical outcomes. The company's biologic processing systems and orthopaedic therapies are gaining traction across the Asia-Pacific region as it prepares for a Nasdaq listing under the ticker INGT.
company.name: Integrant

company.description: Munich-based cleantech startup building autonomous AI workers that integrate with existing renewable energy software (SCADA, maintenance, ERP, weather feeds) to handle alarms, maintenance records, compliance and routine operations on behalf of solar and wind operators. Founded in 2026 by Joseph Perrotta and Kaan Durmaz after 5,000+ interviews with European energy operators surfaced an acute operational-talent shortage; the platform currently manages over 1.8 GW of solar capacity across European portfolios.
company.name: Invertix

company.description: KEMARO is a Swiss robotics company that develops fully autonomous industrial floor-cleaning robots, with its flagship K900 designed for complex, high-debris environments such as logistics centers and manufacturing plants. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Eschlikon (Thurgau), the company has sold over 1,500 robots to more than 600 industrial clients including Unilever, Nestlé, BMW, and Amazon. KEMARO differentiates with a privacy-by-design approach, deliberately avoiding visual data capture and 360-degree cameras to address industrial-espionage and data-protection concerns.
company.name: KEMARO

company.description: LIS Technologies Inc. (LIST) is a US-based proprietary developer of patented advanced laser uranium enrichment technology. The company's Condensation Repression Isotope Selective Laser Activation (CRISLA) technology uses infrared lasers to selectively excite molecules of desired isotopes, making it the world's only proven US-origin and patented laser uranium enrichment method. Selected as one of six domestic companies for the DOE's Low-Enriched Uranium (LEU) Enrichment Acquisition Program, which allocates up to $3.4 billion over ten years, the company is developing a commercial laser enrichment facility at the historic K-25 Site in Oak Ridge, Tennessee.
company.name: LIS Technologies

company.description: Lestari Cooling Energy is a joint-venture platform that develops, finances, operates and maintains district cooling and large-scale chiller systems for large facilities in Malaysia. The company offers zero-CAPEX Cooling-as-a-Service (CaaS), turnkey plant and chiller solutions, and 24/7 operations with central command monitoring to deliver energy savings and high reliability. Backed by infrastructure expertise and integrated design-build-operate capabilities, Lestari targets customers such as data centres, campuses and mixed-use developments seeking lower costs and reduced emissions.
company.name: Lestari Cooling Energy Sdn. Bhd.

company.description: Linexa is a Munich-based industrial AI startup developing a platform that decodes legacy programmable logic controller (PLC) code across vendors and translates it into a unified data model spanning PLC, HMI, and SCADA systems. The platform gives manufacturers end-to-end visibility into their automation logic and uses AI agents to monitor production lines and flag risks before unplanned downtime, accelerating changeovers between products. Founded in late 2025 by Viktor Stryczek, Alexandros Vassiliadis, and Tobias Drees out of Wayra, Telefonica's Munich innovation hub.
company.name: Linexa

company.description: Manako Labs is a London-based Physical AI company building a Vision Agent platform that turns existing camera networks into real-time operational intelligence. Its system lets organizations across industrial, logistics, retail, and infrastructure settings detect issues, coordinate responses, and automate workflows without deploying new hardware or specialist AI teams. Manako also operates Score (Subnet 44), one of the leading subnets on the Bittensor decentralized AI network.
company.name: Manako Labs

company.description: Mantle8 is a French geoscience company pioneering natural hydrogen exploration. The company combines geology, geophysics and geochemistry data to create predictive multiphysics imaging models (HOREX®) for detecting and characterizing natural hydrogen systems. It targets energy and clean-tech markets, aiming to industrialize its technology platform and enable faster, larger-scale exploration. Mantle8 is headquartered in Grenoble and builds on the region's established geoscience expertise.
company.name: Mantle8

company.description: Mesabi Metallics (an Essar Group company) is developing the first new iron ore mine and pellet plant in Minnesota in nearly 50 years. The company is constructing a 7 million metric ton per year facility to produce direct-reduction grade iron ore pellets, supporting US steel supply chain and green steel transition.
company.name: Mesabi Metallics

company.description: MiRus is a privately-held medical device company headquartered in Marietta, Georgia, developing biomaterials, implants and procedural solutions for cardiovascular, structural heart, spine and orthopedic conditions. Its lead cardiovascular program is the SIEGEL balloon-expandable transcatheter aortic valve, which uses a proprietary nitric-oxide-coated rhenium alloy frame and is delivered through an 8 French sheath roughly half the size of currently marketed TAVR sheaths.
company.name: MiRus

company.description: MicroCyto (Weiyuan Synthesis) is a Beijing-based synthetic-biology company founded in 2022 that makes bio-based raw materials for pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, food, feed, and industrial materials. Its AI-driven biocomputation platform handles strain and pathway design; current SKUs include sugar alcohols, rare sugars, natural pigments, and active molecules produced via green biomanufacturing. The team published a collaborative AI-biocomputing platform at ICLR 2026.
company.name: MicroCyto (Weiyuan Synthesis)

company.description: Mind Robotics is an industrial AI and robotics startup building AI-powered robots for manufacturing and industrial applications. The company develops systems focused on dexterity, adaptation, and physical reasoning to automate factory floors, logistics, and supply chains, prioritizing practical, non-humanoid robot designs. It leverages operational data from automotive manufacturing partners to train and deploy robots tailored to real-world industrial tasks. Headquarters are reported in Palo Alto, with operational ties to multiple manufacturing sites.
company.name: Mind Robotics

company.description: Mykor is a Bristol-based biotech startup turning industrial and agricultural waste into low-carbon construction materials. Its biofabrication platform combines engineered mycelium strains, green chemistry and closed-loop automated manufacturing to produce insulation and prefabricated wall systems. The first product, MykoSIP, is a preassembled partition wall that the company says delivers roughly 50% carbon savings versus incumbent systems while meeting fire, acoustic and cost requirements.
company.name: Mykor

company.description: NEURA Robotics is a German high-tech company pioneering cognitive and humanoid robotics, developing AI-powered robots that work seamlessly with humans in industries such as manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare. Founded in 2019, it has rapidly grown to over 300 employees and leads in cognitive cobots and humanoid robots.
company.name: NEURA Robotics

company.description: NOC Energy builds hybrid industrial heating systems that let cement and glass factories switch between electricity and fossil fuels on the fly. Its induction coil technology runs at lower temperatures than conventional alternatives, letting plants exploit cheap grid electricity while keeping fossil infrastructure as backup. The Houston-based climatetech startup is incubated at Greentown Labs and is running pilots with multinational cement and glass manufacturers.
company.name: NOC Energy

company.description: NanoZymeX is a nanobiotechnology startup spun out of the University of Basel, developing next-generation enzyme replacement therapies using proprietary lipid-based nanoparticles. The company's platform improves the delivery of therapeutic enzymes directly into target cells and lysosomes, increasing functional activity in affected tissues while reducing immune reactions. NanoZymeX targets lysosomal storage disorders including Pompe disease, representing a multibillion-dollar therapeutic market.
company.name: NanoZymeX
